preamble: The lenition or debuccalization of a velar fricative /x/ into a glottal fricative /h/, a common process of phonetic weakening.

examples.note: Debuccalization of /x/ in many Latin American dialects.
Compare German 'Licht' /lɪçt/ with English 'light' (where /x/ became /h/ before being lost).

sources: Hualde, J. I. (2005). The Sounds of Spanish.
